name: Illinois v. Gates
summary: Established totality of circumstances test for probable cause; replaced rigid two-prong Aguilar-Spinelli test with flexible standard considering all information known to officers.
holding: Probable cause determined by totality of circumstances; magistrate makes practical, commonsense decision whether fair probability exists that contraband or evidence will be found; informant reliability and basis of knowledge considered but not separate rigid requirements.
relevance_to_audit: Establishes flexible standard for probable cause; relevant to analyzing basis for administrative searches, enforcement actions, and investigative determinations.
